things.name: Old Oswestry Hill Fort
things.description: One of Britain's best-preserved Iron Age hill forts dating from 800 BC with spectacular multiple ramparts rising over the Shropshire plain

things.name: Oswestry Town Centre
things.description: Attractive market town with a medieval street layout, independent shops and the famous weekly Wednesday and Saturday markets in the town square

things.name: Wilfred Owen's Birthplace
things.description: Birthplace of WWI poet Wilfred Owen in Oswestry; the town commemorates its most famous son with a museum and memorial garden honouring his wartime verse

things.name: Offa's Dyke Path
things.description: Long-distance national trail running from Chepstow to Prestatyn along the Welsh-English border, starting near Oswestry with spectacular ridge-top walking

things.name: Chirk Castle (National Trust)
things.description: Medieval border castle built in 1295 still inhabited after 700 years, with sumptuous state rooms, magnificent formal gardens and parkland

things.name: Pontcysyllte Aqueduct (UNESCO)
things.description: Thomas Telford's breathtaking 1805 iron aqueduct carrying the Llangollen Canal 38 metres above the Dee Valley; a UNESCO World Heritage masterpiece

things.name: Oswestry Bicycle Museum
things.description: Fascinating museum celebrating the bicycle and the remarkable industrial heritage of Oswestry as a Victorian manufacturing town

things.name: Lake Vyrnwy
things.description: Victorian reservoir in the Berwyn Mountains 25km from Oswestry surrounded by moorland and forest; cycling, walking and birdwatching in wild Welsh scenery

things.name: St. Oswald's Church
things.description: Medieval parish church at the heart of Oswestry dedicated to the Anglo-Saxon king Oswald, with a striking tower and a churchyard of historic interest

things.name: Park Hall Stadium
things.description: Home of The New Saints FC, Wales's most successful club and regular UEFA Europa and Conference League qualifiers with multiple Welsh Premier League titles
